reason: The item cleanly targets method-/definition-dependence of “thermal runaway temperature” and forces boundary-aware admissibility reasoning for safety standards rather than mechanistic or numerical computation. Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible (error attribution, “true” ARC privileging, gradient mechanism, correction-factor unification), creating real expert discrimination. Minor redundancy among correct options and some wording asymmetries (“inadmissible,” “true onset,” “fixed correction factor”) slightly reduce neutrality and invite test-taking heuristics.

The item cleanly targets boundary-aware evaluation of two proposed safety inferences (predictive value of early vent gas and efficacy of suppression for HF hazard) and constrains the mechanistic basis tightly to phase-gated HF appearance and fire-independence. Decoy space is implicitly handled via the need to reject common but incorrect causal linkages (early vent as precursor signal; HF as combustion product), though as a short-answer item it relies on rubric completeness rather than option-level traps, slightly reducing discrimination against fluent but shallow responses.
